title: isodon excisus var. shikokianus
intro: Isodon excisus var. shikokianus, commonly known as Japanese goldenroot, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help to relieve symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Japanese goldenroot include reducing fever, promoting digestion, and easing symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, it is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and is also used in traditional remedies for its antiseptic and expectorant properties.
